title: Kenneth Lau | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:

A dedicated Fragomen Partner, Kenny is entrusted with overseeing the firm’s Asia Pacific Coordination Center (APCC) and is actively involved in managing various offices across the region.
In this capacity, Kenny supervises not only several of Fragomen’s Southeast Asia practices and countries but also extends his purview to other markets in the broader region, such as Japan and Korea, as well as countries in South Asia and the Pacific Islands. His deep involvement in engaging local immigration firms in countries where the firm partners with them ensures seamless transactional work when collaborating across borders.
At a higher level, Kenny assumes responsibility for understanding diverse immigration regimes within the region. This uniquely allows him and his team to have a comparative view of the region, allowing the team to have a deeper understanding of the various countries and to share such knowledge and insight across markets; for instance, from a government strategies perspective but also in providing strategic advice to clients for their business planning and expansion requirements.
As a multi-jurisdictional and multi-country strategist, Kenny offers invaluable insights to clients, aiding them in their decision-making processes concerning market entry and growth strategies. Leveraging his extensive client service background, Kenny emphasizes the importance of offering trusted recommendations rather than merely inundating clients with options. He instills this ethos within his team through ongoing training and encourages them to adapt advice according to each client’s unique needs.
Kenny leads by example, demonstrating dedication and commitment by consistently exceeding expectations and constant learning. By fostering a supportive environment and nurturing long-term relationships with his team members, many of whom he has worked with for more than a decade, Kenny’s goal is to ensure that his team of diverse individuals feels valued and supported.

- The University of California College of the Law, San Francisco, J.D., 2006
- University of California, Berkeley, B.A., with High Distinction , 2003
Bar Admissions: California
Phi Beta Kappa, 2004

### Professional accomplishments
Member, American Bar Association (Section of Business Law; Section of International Law; Young Lawyers Division)
Member, American Immigration Lawyers Association
Member, California State Bar Association
Coordinated the immigration needs for the transition of employees as a result of both large and small mergers and acquisitions.
Assisted various clients with their project moves, coordinating a group of individuals into countries such as Thailand, the Philippines and Indonesia.
Advised and assisted clients with the immigration-related needs of starting up a new branch in a foreign country.

### Publications and lectures
Speaker, “Fragomen’s Asia Pacific Conference 2026 - Shaping the Future: Solutions Transforming Immigration and Mobility.” Fragomen , Singapore, Singapore, 11 March 2026.
Speaker, Fragomen’s Global Japan Conference 2025 , Osaka, Japan, 11 November 2025.
Speaker, “Immigration in a Rapidly Changing World: What You Need to Know and Why.” Fragomen , Singapore, Singapore, 9 September 2025.
Speaker, “Unpacked: DOLE Order 248 and its Impact on Hiring Foreign Talent.” Fragomen , Webinar, 7 May 2025.
Author, “Ray of Hope: Vietnam’s Resolution 105.” Fragomen Blog , October 6, 2021.
Co-author, “Bubble within a Bubble: The Phuket Sandbox.” Fragomen Blog, July 12, 2021.
Speaker, “The Flickering Torch: The Olympics and Japan’s Covid-Impacted Immigration Policy.” Fragomen Podcast: Asia Pacific Insight Series , July 14, 2021.
Speaker, “Southeast Asia at a Crossroads: COVID-19’s Impact in Protectionist Times,” Fragomen Webcast , June 1, 2021.
Author, “Storm Clouds Ahead.” Fragomen Blog , April 29, 2021.
Co-author, “One Year On: The current impact of Covid-19 on Vietnam’s immigration system, Fragomen on Immigration.” Fragomen Blog , February 25, 2021.
Speaker, “Immigration in the Asia Pacific.” Fragomen Virtual Conference , November 11, 2020.
Speaker, Global Mobility Executive Leaders Exchange VX: Far Asia Webinar , October 27, 2020.
Speaker, “Your Global Distributed Workforce and the Future of Mobility.” Global Mobility Executive Leaders Exchange VX: Singapore Webinar , September 24, 2020.
Speaker, “The Double-Edged Sword: Japan Immigration’s Balancing Act During COVID-19, Part 2 (Japanese).” Fragomen Podcast: Asia Pacific Insight Series , August 24, 2020.
Speaker, “The Double-Edged Sword: Japan Immigration’s Balancing Act During COVID-19, Part 1 (English).” Fragomen Podcast: Asia Pacific Insight Series , August 20, 2020.
Speaker, “The New Ab(Normal) in Korean Immigration.”  Fragomen Podcast: Asia Pacific Insight Series, July 2020.
Speaker, “Southeast Asia Remobilising the Workforce Post COVID-19.” Fragomen Webcast , July 8, 2020.
Co-author, “Heavy-handed Yet flexible: The Thai Government’s Adaptability in the Face of COVID-19.” Fragomen Blog , July 8, 2020.
Co-author, “Bubbling to the Surface: Vietnam’s Cautious (but Optimistic) Approach to Reopening.” Fragomen Blog , June 11, 2020.
“Disruptors Revisited: A retrospective look at Fragomen’s 2019 APAC Conference from the ASEAN perspective.” Fragomen Blog, April 29, 2020.
Speaker, Global Mobility Executive Leaders Exchange VX | APAC Webinar , April 23, 2020.
Speaker, “Singapore and Asia Pacific Coordination Centre Immigration Impacts of COVID-19.” Fragomen Webcast , April 8, 2020.
Co-author, “Managing Immigration in the Age of Disruption: A Japanese system keeping up with the times.” Fragomen Blog, August 27, 2019.
Co-author, “Managing Immigration in the Age of Disruption: A look at Indonesia.” Fragomen Blog, August 5, 2019.
Co-author, “Managing Immigration in the Age of Disruption: South Korea in the Fourth Industrial Revolution.” Fragomen Blog , August 5, 2019.
“Fragomen Partner on Korean Immigration Landscape and Global Compliance Trends, Interview with the Korea Legal Times .” May 21, 2019.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
